hero_subtitle: SFTY connects wallet and transaction data with risk signals to help compliance teams review and document high-risk source-of-funds activity with audit-ready traceability.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Source of funds reviews depend on manual steps
problem_description: Compliance teams face delays and inconsistent risk assessments when source-of-funds data remains dispersed across multiple tools and providers. This fragmentation complicates linking wallet activity and transaction risk to compliance cases, slowing review and weakening audit readiness.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY connects wallet activity, transaction risk signals and provider outputs into unified source-of-funds case workflows that improve review efficiency and preserve audit-ready evidence for compliance teams.
workflow_title: From wallet risk to source of funds review
workflow_description: SFTY aggregates wallet and transaction risk data, routes high-risk source-of-funds alerts into case workflows, supports evidence collection and enables documented compliance decisions.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond wallet alerts. Structured source-of-funds control
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects fragmented wallet and transaction risk signals into structured reviews, cases, decisions and audit-ready evidence for high-risk source-of-funds compliance workflows.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs in managing high-risk source-of-funds reviews with integrated provider workflows, audit-grade evidence, and controlled deployment for defensible bank due diligence.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY support high-risk source-of-funds reviews
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how transaction monitoring links to source-of-funds cases
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit-ready evidence supports source-of-funds decisions
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe provider orchestration for wallet risk and sanctions exposure
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: How does SFTY help review high-risk source of funds?
faq_1_answer: SFTY aggregates wallet and transaction data to identify high-risk source-of-funds scenarios, structuring alerts, cases and decisions into a single compliance workflow for VASP teams.
faq_2_question: How are source-of-funds alerts managed in workflows?
faq_2_answer: Source-of-funds alerts are routed into structured review workflows with case creation, decision logging and escalation based on configured risk thresholds and internal policies.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve source-of-funds evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves audit-ready evidence including provider outputs, case notes, decision logs and linked wallet and transaction history for defensible source-of-funds review.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY connect existing wallet and transaction providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY connects multiple KYT, blockchain analytics and transaction monitoring providers to connect their outputs within a controlled compliance operating layer.
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY sits above providers and structures their outputs but does not replace source-of-funds or KYT providers.
final_description: See how SFTY connects wallet risk, transaction data and provider outputs into audit-ready source-of-funds cases.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
